preconditions: The MII template and all three no-op encodings are legal
inputs: A 128-bit MII bundle with independently chosen non-overlapping template and slot payload bits
expected_results: Host-side literal bit extraction recovers the exact template and three 41-bit slots
The same packed bundle executes to its completion marker
allowed_results: The exact four extracted fields and successful guest completion
required_unchanged_state: Memory outside the runner queues and scratch range
cases.committed_prefix: Bundle extraction alone commits no instruction result
cases.restart_action: Not exercised; fetch interruptions retry under the applicable interruption rule

oracle.derivation: Literal shifts and masks from the cited architectural bit ranges; no production decoder table is imported

citation.anchor: Template Field Encoding and Instruction Slot Mapping

preconditions: Each slot contains a legal no-op for its architecturally assigned unit
MLX uses the architecturally defined long no-op form
expected_results: Every defined template executes with its literal M/I/F/B/L/X unit assignment and stop placement
The guest reports a count of 24 completed templates
cases.committed_prefix: Only the legal no-op instructions and final count marker complete

oracle.derivation: The legal template/unit/stop matrix is a literal test-owned transcription of the cited table

preconditions: The stream contains adjacent aligned bundles
Destination registers are independent and the groups are dependency-correct
inputs: Two current-IP reads in one MII bundle followed by a current-IP read in the next bundle
expected_results: Both reads in the first bundle observe its address
The following read observes the address increased by 16
allowed_results: The literal sum of the three expected IP values
cases.committed_prefix: All three nonfaulting IP reads complete in sequential bundle order

oracle.derivation: Expected IPs are calculated from test-owned bundle addresses and the architectural 16-byte bundle size

preconditions: The producer and consumer are separated by a template stop
inputs: Producer writes 0x33 to a GR; consumer in the next group copies that GR
expected_results: The consumer reads 0x33
cases.committed_prefix: The producer update completes before the consumer group reads

oracle.derivation: The expected literal is set before the architectural stop and compared after it

preconditions: The group uses legal WAR rather than prohibited RAW or WAW
The source GR contains 0x11 at group entry
inputs: An early instruction reads the source GR; a later instruction in the same group writes 0x22 to it
expected_results: The read returns group-entry value 0x11
The group completes with the source GR updated to 0x22
permitted_state_changes: The WAR source and readback GRs
cases.committed_prefix: At group completion the legal write is visible, while the earlier read retains the group-entry image

oracle.derivation: Literal entry and replacement values exercise the manual's legal WAR rule without relying on same-group RAW behavior

citation.anchor: Exceptions are reported in instruction order

preconditions: Each faulting group satisfies the resource dependency rules
Interruption collection is enabled for exact saved-state checks
The repair handler changes only the missing backing word and saved PSR.rt
inputs: Illegal-operation faults in slots 1 and 2 with observable instructions before and after
A real alternate-data-TLB miss during a mandatory return fill followed by RFI retry
expected_results: Only instructions before each fault commit
Later instructions do not update state
Saved IIP/IPSR.ri/ISR/IIPA identify the fault
Repair and RFI retry consume the replacement word without replaying committed work
allowed_results: Exact literal prefix, interruption, replacement, and terminal state described by the oracle
permitted_state_changes: Return-frame and RSE state required by successful retry
required_unchanged_state: Already committed prefix values during retry
Memory outside the declared program and repair ranges
fault_priority: Earliest enabled faulting instruction in program order
Illegal-operation validation precedes later instruction effects
cases.committed_prefix: All prior instructions update state; the faulting and subsequent instructions do not; retry does not replay the prefix
cases.restart_action: The handler repairs the backing word, clears saved PSR.rt, executes RFI, and resumes the same faulting return operation

oracle.derivation: Literal expected vectors, slot indices, saved registers, prefix markers, replacement word, and final RSE state are derived from the cited sequencing rule and declared test program

preconditions: getf.d is dynamically enabled and selects FR0
expected_results: r30 contains IEEE-754 double +0.0 bits 0x0000000000000000
cases.committed_prefix: Only the destination GR update completes

oracle.derivation: Expected bits are the literal IEEE-754 representation of architectural +0.0

preconditions: getf.d is dynamically enabled and selects FR1
expected_results: r30 contains IEEE-754 double +1.0 bits 0x3ff0000000000000
cases.committed_prefix: Only the destination GR update completes

oracle.derivation: Expected bits are the literal IEEE-754 representation of architectural +1.0

preconditions: The add and tnat instructions are dynamically enabled
inputs: add r30=r0,r0 followed by tnat.z on r30
expected_results: r30 is zero
tnat reports NaT clear
allowed_results: Predicate image contains p0 and the NaT-clear result predicate only
permitted_state_changes: Destination GR and declared predicate pair
cases.committed_prefix: The zero destination and predicate result complete in separate dependency-correct groups
applicable_variants: explicit NaT test of the copied value

oracle.derivation: Expected integer and NaT results are literal architectural constants

preconditions: All writable predicates are cleared before observation
inputs: mov r30=pr and a p0-qualified marker instruction
expected_results: Predicate image bit 0 is one
The p0-qualified instruction executes
cases.committed_prefix: The readback and marker updates complete

result_observations: actual_state packs predicate image and marker as 0x101

oracle.derivation: Expected bit and qualifier values are the literal architectural constant one

citation.anchor: when used as a destination, the result is discarded

preconditions: The selected normal compare legally names p0 as its false destination
inputs: cmp.lt p0,p6=r0,r0 followed by predicate-image readback
expected_results: The attempted false write to p0 is discarded
Subsequent readback contains p0=1 and p6=1
cases.committed_prefix: PR6 updates; the PR0 destination produces no architectural write
applicable_variants: normal integer compare with p0 destination

oracle.derivation: Expected predicate bits follow directly from false p0 discard and true p6 compare result
